message:
perf(cli/blocked): skip config + external-deps work when not needed

`bd blocked` was unconditionally loading config and resolving
external-database dependency status on every invocation, even when
the caller only asked for `--robot` JSON output (which doesn't
care about color, doesn't touch the external-DB walker, and only
needs the local blocked-issues view). On a beads_dir with several
external project dependencies, `resolve_external_dependency_statuses`
walks every external DB path, parses each, and builds a status map
— enough work to make `bd blocked --robot` noticeably slow on cold
caches even though none of that data ends up in the JSON output.

Two changes:

1. **Lazy config load.** `config_layer` is now optional. We load
   it only when:

   - `output_format` is `Text` or `Csv` (those formats consult
     `should_use_color`, which lives on the config), or
   - `storage.has_external_dependencies(true)?` returns true (we
     need the config to enumerate the external DB paths).

   For `--robot` and `--json` invocations on a beads_dir without
   external project dependencies, config loading is skipped
   entirely — that's the most common case for agent traffic and
   was paying the largest config-parse penalty per call.

   The early `has_external_dependencies` probe is cheap (it
   queries `dependencies WHERE …` with a `LIMIT 1`) so we get the
   information without paying the full external-status walk.

2. **`use_color` decoupled from config layer.** Now a two-part
   check: first that the output format is `Text`/`Csv` (other
   formats never style anyway) and only then that the config
   layer is present and reports color enabled. `is_some_and(...)`
   collapses both checks into one expression and means we don't
   have to materialize the config in the no-color JSON path.

3. **External-deps block gated on `has_external_dependencies`.**
   The whole `external_blockers` resolution block is now wrapped
   in `if has_external_dependencies { ... }` so callers without
   external deps don't pay for the external-DB resolution at all.
   The inner code is otherwise identical: same external_db_paths
   enumeration, same `resolve_external_dependency_statuses` call,
   same blocked_issues mutation, same fetch-and-merge of
   external issue rows.

   The `expect("external dependencies require config")` inside
   the block is sound because we only enter the block when
   `has_external_dependencies` is true, and `needs_config` is
   structurally `true` whenever `has_external_dependencies` is
   — so config_layer is guaranteed `Some`.

No semantic change. The output for any given invocation is
identical to before; the path through the function is just cheaper
for the common no-external-deps + JSON case.
